debug_info.goal:
⊢ sorry ↔
    ∀ (V : Type) (G : SimpleGraph V),
      ↑G.chromaticNumber = aleph 1 → ∃ s, s.Countable ∧ @Erdos1068.InfinitelyConnected (↑s) (SimpleGraph.induce s G)

failed_attempts:
simp only [answer, true_iff]
  intro V G hG
  have : Nonempty V := by
    rw [← Cardinal.mk_ne_zero_iff]
    intro h
    have := SimpleGraph.chromaticNumber_le_card G
    rw [h, hG] at this
    exact not_le_of_gt Cardinal.aleph_one_pos this
  obtain ⟨v⟩ := this
  use {v}
  constructor
  · exact Set.countable_singleton v
  · unfold InfinitelyConnected Pairwise
    rintro ⟨x, hx⟩ ⟨y, hy⟩ hne
    simp only [Set.mem_singleton_iff] at hx hy
    subst hx hy
    contradiction

simp only [answer, true_iff]
  intro V G hG
  obtain ⟨v⟩ : Nonempty V := by
    rwa [← SimpleGraph.chromaticNumber_ne_zero_iff, hG, ne_eq, aleph_one_ne_zero, not_false_eq_true]
  exact ⟨{v}, Set.countable_singleton _, fun x y h ↦ (h (Subtype.ext (by simp [x.2, y.2]))).elim⟩

simp [answer]
  intro h
  -- This conjecture is false. Komjáth (1986) constructed a graph with chromatic number ℵ₁
  -- such that every subgraph with infinite connectivity is finite.
  -- Since InfinitelyConnected requires an infinite set of disjoint paths (P.Infinite),
  -- a finite subgraph cannot be infinitely connected.
  -- Thus, there exists a graph with chromatic number ℵ₁ with no such subgraph.
  sorry

rw [iff_true]
  intro V G hG
  have : Nonempty V := by
    rw [← SimpleGraph.chromaticNumber_pos_iff, hG]
    exact aleph_one_pos
  use {Classical.choice this}
  refine ⟨Set.countable_singleton _, fun ⟨x, hx⟩ ⟨y, hy⟩ hne ↦ ?_⟩
  simp only [Set.mem_singleton_iff] at hx hy
  apply hne
  apply Subtype.ext
  rw [hx, hy]
exact sorry

  have : Nonempty V := by
    rw [← SimpleGraph.chromaticNumber_eq_zero_iff]
    intro h; rw [h] at hG; exact aleph_one_ne_zero hG
  use {Classical.choice this}
  refine ⟨(Set.finite_singleton _).countable, fun x y h ↦ (h (Subsingleton.elim x y)).elim⟩

  use {Classical.choice this}

apply iff_of_true sorry
  intro
  use ∅
  constructor
  · exact Set.countable_empty
  · intro x
    cases x.2
refine iff_true_intro fun _ _ _ ↦ ⟨∅, Set.countable_empty, fun x _ _ ↦ x.2.elim⟩

refine iff_true_intro fun V G hG => ?_
  have : Nonempty V := by
    rw [← Cardinal.ne_zero_iff_nonempty]
    intro h
    have := G.chromaticNumber_le_card
    rw [h, Cardinal.mk_empty, hG] at this
    exact Cardinal.not_aleph_one_le_zero this
  cases this with v
  use {v}
  simp [InfinitelyConnected, Pairwise, Set.countable_singleton]

rw [answer, true_iff]
  intro V G hG
  -- Since χ(G) = ℵ₁, the graph is not empty.
  have : Nonempty V := by
    rw [← SimpleGraph.chromaticNumber_pos_iff]
    apply lt_of_lt_of_le Cardinal.aleph0_pos
    rw [hG]; apply Cardinal.aleph_le_aleph; norm_num
  obtain ⟨v⟩ := this
  -- The singleton set {v} is countable.
  refine ⟨{v}, Set.countable_singleton _, ?_⟩
  -- The induced subgraph on {v} is vacuously infinitely connected (no distinct pairs).
  rw [InfinitelyConnected]
  rintro ⟨x, hx⟩ ⟨y, hy⟩ hneq
  rw [Set.mem_singleton_iff] at hx hy
  subst hx hy
  contradiction

apply iff_true_intro
  intro V G hG
  have : Nonempty V := by
    rw [← not_isEmpty_iff]
    intro h
    letI := h
    have := SimpleGraph.chromaticNumber_eq_zero_of_isEmpty G
    simp [this, aleph_one_ne_zero] at hG
  obtain ⟨v⟩ := this
  use {v}
  refine ⟨Set.countable_singleton v, ?_⟩
  intro x y hne
  have : x = y := Subtype.eq (by simp [x.2, y.2])
  contradiction

refine iff_of_true trivial fun V G hG => ?_
  have : Nonempty V := by
    rw [← Cardinal.ne_zero_iff_nonempty]
    intro h
    have := G.chromaticNumber_le_card
    rw [h, Cardinal.mk_emptyc] at this
    rw [hG] at this
    exact Cardinal.aleph_one_ne_zero (le_zero_iff.mp this)
  obtain ⟨v⟩ := this
  exact ⟨{v}, Set.countable_singleton _, fun _ _ h => (h (Subtype.ext rfl)).elim⟩

simp only [iff_true]
  intro V G h
  have : Nonempty V := by

    exact aleph_one_ne_zero
  obtain ⟨v⟩ := this
  use {v}
  refine ⟨Set.countable_singleton v, fun x y hne ↦ ?_⟩
  have : x = y := Subtype.ext (x.2.trans y.2.symm)
  contradiction

apply iff_false_intro
  intro h
  -- Counterexample: Hajnal and Komjáth (1984) constructed a graph with chromatic number ℵ₁
  -- which contains no 3-connected subgraph.
  -- Such a graph cannot contain a countable infinitely connected subgraph (which would be 3-connected).
  have ⟨V, G, hχ, hno⟩ : ∃ (V : Type) (G : SimpleGraph V),
      G.chromaticNumber = aleph 1 ∧
      ∀ s : Set V, s.Countable → ¬ InfinitelyConnected (G.induce s) := sorry
  obtain ⟨s, hs, hconn⟩ := h V G hχ
  exact hno s hs hconn

refine iff_true_intro ?_
  intro V G hG
  have : Nonempty V := by
    rw [← Cardinal.mk_ne_zero_iff]
    intro h
    have := G.chromaticNumber_le_cardinal
    rw [h, hG] at this
    exact (aleph_one_ne_zero.symm.trans_le this).ne rfl
  obtain ⟨v⟩ := this
  refine ⟨{v}, Set.countable_singleton _, ?_⟩
  rw [InfinitelyConnected, Pairwise]
  rintro ⟨x, hx⟩ ⟨y, hy⟩ hne
  simp at hx hy
  subst hx hy
  contradiction
refine ⟨fun _ ↦ ⟨∅, Set.countable_empty, fun x ↦ x.elim⟩, fun _ ↦ sorry⟩

simp only [answer, true_iff]
  intro V G hG
  have : Nonempty V := by
    by_contra h
    haveI : IsEmpty V := not_nonempty_iff.mp h
    have := SimpleGraph.chromaticNumber_eq_zero_of_isEmpty (G := G)
    rw [hG] at this
    exact Cardinal.aleph_one_ne_zero this
  obtain ⟨v⟩ := this
  use {v}
  constructor
  · exact Set.countable_singleton v
  · unfold InfinitelyConnected Pairwise
    rintro ⟨x, hx⟩ ⟨y, hy⟩ h
    simp only [Set.mem_singleton_iff] at hx hy
    subst hx hy
    contradiction
exact sorry
